title: Dropbox token refresh architecture rewritten as self-contained — eliminated fragile subprocess dependency
symptom: Dropbox uploads and reads failed silently. API calls returned errors but reported success in some paths. Token was expired with no auto-refresh.
root_cause: dropbox-cli.py depended on a separate dropbox-auth.py script via subprocess call for token refresh. When files were reorganized into scripts/legacy/, the auto-refresh path broke. The 4-hour access token expired and was never renewed.
fix: Rewrote dropbox-cli.py to be fully self-contained. Token refresh is now a single inline HTTP call to Dropbox OAuth endpoint (~200ms). All credentials read from .dropbox_auth.json (chmod 600). No external script dependencies.
lesson: Token refresh logic must live in the same file as the API calls. Never depend on external scripts for auth — they can move, be deleted, or fail silently. Refresh token is permanent; access token is 4-hour. Always verify file exists AND has non-zero size before upload/send.
prevention: For any CLI tool that uses short-lived tokens: inline the refresh logic. Test by forcing token expiry and confirming transparent refresh. Add 'dropbox-cli.py status' to smoke test suite.
